title: Improve story narration: remove speaker labels, add per-character voice settings, and update Miles visual description

requirements.text: Update narration script generation to avoid explicit speaker labels (e.g., avoid phrases like "Miles says:" / "Jefferson says:" / "Miles thinks:") while still narrating dialogue and thoughts in a natural way.

quotes: maybe don't say so and so says

acceptanceCriteria: When narrating any panel part of type "dialogue", the spoken line does not contain the substring " says:" and does not prepend the speaker name by default.
When narrating any panel part of type "thought", the spoken line does not contain the substring " thinks:" and does not prepend the speaker name by default.
Captions, scene text, and SFX continue to be narrated in a reasonable, understandable way (no runtime errors; narration still produces a non-empty script for the existing story).

requirements.text: Add a narration “Voice Style” configuration that lets the user choose different text-to-speech voices (and basic voice parameters like rate and pitch if supported) for key speakers (at minimum: Miles, Jefferson, and a default for all other characters), and apply those selections during playback.

quotes: it has to sound like miles and his did and the other characters

acceptanceCriteria: The UI includes a section in the Story Narration area for selecting a voice for Miles, a voice for Jefferson, and a default voice for all other speakers.
If the browser provides multiple speechSynthesis voices, the dropdowns list them and selecting a voice changes the actual SpeechSynthesisUtterance.voice used during narration.
If the browser provides no selectable voices (or voices are not yet loaded), the UI shows a clear English message and narration still works with the browser default voice.
Changing voice settings does not require page reload and takes effect for the next started narration session.
The implementation does not claim to perfectly imitate copyrighted character voices; it only uses available system/browser TTS voices.

requirements.text: Update story panel metadata so Miles is visually described as Black with curly hair in relevant illustration alt text (at least the opening Miles panel).

quotes: miles has curly hair and is black

acceptanceCriteria: In the first story panel illustrationAlt (spiderverse-panel-01...), the description explicitly mentions Miles is Black and has curly hair.
No story dialogue content is removed; the line "Why am I stuck to the ceiling?!" remains present in the data and continues to be narrated.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only).
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
Backend remains a single Motoko actor; do not introduce additional backend services.
nonGoals: Do not implement third-party or AI voice cloning/impersonation.
Do not add external TTS providers or APIs.
Do not change the overall story structure beyond the requested Miles visual description and narration formatting/voice controls.
